World.headline: West Bengal Election Results 2026: Counting Underway, BJP Ahead in Early Trends
World.summary: Vote counting for the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections is underway across 293 constituencies, with the BJP currently leading in early trends against the incumbent TMC. Security is tight at 77 counting centers following a high 92.47% voter turnout. The focus remains on whether the All India Trinamool Congress can retain power or if the Bharatiya Janata Party can make a significant breakthrough in the state.
key_points: Counting for 293 West Bengal Assembly seats is in progress.
BJP is leading in early trends, challenging TMC's bid for a fourth term.
Voter turnout was 92.47%, the highest since Independence.
Strict security measures are in place at 77 counting centers.

World.headline: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026: DMK Leads in Early Trends Amidst Three-Way Contest
World.summary: Vote counting has begun for the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, with the DMK currently leading in early trends across 234 constituencies. A high voter turnout of over 84.80% and the emergence of Vijay's TVK as a third force add unpredictability to the outcome. Exit polls have offered mixed projections, reflecting the complexity of the contest.
key_points: Counting is underway for all 234 Tamil Nadu Assembly seats.
DMK is showing early leads, though exit polls are divided.
The election saw an 84.80% turnout, one of the highest in state history.
Vijay's TVK party has introduced a new layer of competition in the traditional DMK-AIADMK rivalry.

World.headline: Assam Election Results 2026: BJP-Led NDA Takes Early Lead in Vote Counting
World.summary: Counting of votes has commenced for all 126 Assembly seats in Assam, with early trends indicating a lead for the BJP-led NDA. The election, marked by identity politics and welfare issues, will determine if the NDA secures a third consecutive term or if the Congress makes a comeback. Exit poll projections largely point towards an advantage for the BJP-led alliance.
key_points: Vote counting for 126 Assam constituencies has started.
Early trends suggest a comfortable lead for the BJP-led NDA.
Over 700 candidates are in the fray, including several prominent political figures.
The outcome will test BJP's dominance and the opposition's ability to regain ground.

World.headline: Kerala Election Results 2026: UDF Leads in Early Trends, NDA Shows Presence
World.summary: Vote counting is underway for Kerala's 140 Assembly constituencies, with early trends showing the UDF in the lead. The election, marked by a high 78.27% turnout, signifies a shift towards a triangular contest with the BJP-led NDA gaining ground in certain seats like Palakkad and Nemom. Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan is seeking a rare third consecutive term.
key_points: Counting for 140 Kerala Assembly seats has begun.
UDF is leading in early trends; NDA shows strength in Palakkad and Nemom.
Voter turnout was 78.27%, indicating strong participation.
The election reflects a shift towards a triangular contest with NDA as a third force.

World.headline: Puducherry Election Results 2026: NDA Eyes Second Term as Counting Commences
World.summary: Counting of votes has started for 30 constituencies in Puducherry following a historic 89.87% voter turnout, the highest since its merger with India. The ruling NDA, comprising AINRC and BJP, is vying for a second term against the Congress-DMK alliance and new entrant TVK. Chief Minister N Rangasamy is pitted against Congress leader V Vaithilingam in the high-profile Thattanchavady seat.
key_points: Vote counting is underway for 30 Puducherry Assembly constituencies.
The election saw a historic 89.87% voter turnout.
The ruling NDA aims for a second term against the Congress-DMK alliance and TVK.

World.headline: Bypoll Election Results 2026: Counting Begins for Seven Assembly Seats Across Five States
World.summary: Vote counting has commenced for by-elections across seven assembly constituencies in Karnataka, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Tripura, and Nagaland. These polls fill vacancies left by deceased MLAs, with significant attention on Maharashtra's Baramati, where Sunetra Pawar (Ajit Pawar's wife) is contesting. The results will determine new representatives and offer key political signals ahead of future electoral contests.

Vacancies were caused by the deaths of sitting MLAs.

These results could impact national political equations ahead of future contests.
